Prior ProvisionsA prior , title II, , , provided for judicial review for workers or groups aggrieved by a final determination by the Secretary under , and was classified to , prior to repeal by .section 250 of Pub. L. 93–61888 Stat. 2029section 2273 of this titlesection 2322 of this titlePub. L. 96–417Jan. 3, 1975

Effective Date of Repeal, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–210, div. A, title I, § 123(c)116 Stat. 944Aug. 6, 2002“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending sections 2275 and 2395 of this title and repealing this subpart and ] shall apply with respect to petitions filed under chapter 2 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974 [this part], on or after the date that is 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act [].section 2322 of this titleAug. 6, 2002“(2).—Workers certified as eligible before effective dateNotwithstanding subsection (a), a worker receiving benefits under chapter 2 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974 shall continue to receive (or be eligible to receive) benefits and services under chapter 2 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974, as in effect on the day before the amendments made by this section take effect under subsection (a), for any week for which the worker meets the eligibility requirements of such chapter 2 as in effect on such date.”
